methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $250M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $300.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Liquid AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2.35B

private.name: Field AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

data.narrative:
Twelve Labs, founded by Jae Lee and Aiden Lee, is a Series A AI & Software company seeking valuation guidance on its $250M post-money valuation after $57M raised to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 10 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies, comprising 1 public comparables (Palantir, PLTR) and 8 private benchmarks including OpenAI, Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence, World Labs, and others.

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier from the current $250M post-money valuation, implying a forward valuation of $500M at the next funding round. Peer-median analysis across the 10-company comparables set yields a median valuation of $3.7B, with a range spanning $500M to $300B. This wide range reflects the heterogeneous maturity and capital efficiency across the peer cohort, though the median is anchored by less early-stage outliers like OpenAI and more recent late-stage entrants like Safe Superintelligence ($32B Series B valuation).

Synthesizing both methods yields a triangulated valuation range of $425M (low) to $4.2B (high), with a median of $2.1B. The considerable distance between stage-progression ($500M) and peer-median ($3.7B) reflects uncertainty around Twelve Labs' product-market fit trajectory relative to its peer set. Key risks include whether revenue metrics and user engagement will support peer-level valuation expansion, and the composition sensitivity of the comparables to outlier-stage companies at Series B/later funding.
